Free-form assignment field used for additional sorting, grouping, or reconciliation purposes (e.g., invoice number, payment reference).
The business area or division for cross-company code reporting and segment analysis.
The date and time when this journal entry record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and compliance.
The credit amount for this journal entry line in the document currency. Zero if this is a debit entry.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the journal entry amounts are denominated.
The debit amount for this journal entry line in the document currency. Zero if this is a credit entry.
The date on the source document or invoice that originated this journal entry. May differ from posting date for period-end adjustments.
The externally-known unique document number assigned to this journal entry in the general ledger system. Used for audit trail and reference in financial reports.
Classification of the journal entry document type. SA=Standard Accrual, AA=Asset Accounting, KR=Vendor Invoice, DR=Customer Invoice, AB=Accounting Document, RV=Reversal Document.
Current lifecycle status of the journal entry. Draft=not yet posted, Posted=finalized in GL, Parked=saved but not posted, Reversed=reversed by another entry, Cancelled=voided.
The foreign exchange rate used to convert document currency amounts to local currency. Applicable for multi-currency transactions.
The type of exchange rate used for currency conversion. Spot=transaction date rate, Average=period average, Budget=budgeted rate, Closing=period-end rate.
The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year to which this journal entry is assigned. Typically 1-12 for monthly periods, or 13-16 for special closing periods.
The fiscal year to which this journal entry is assigned for financial reporting purposes.
Free-form descriptive text at the document header level explaining the purpose or context of the journal entry.
Flag indicating whether this journal entry represents an intercompany transaction requiring elimination during consolidation.
Free-form descriptive text at the line item level providing additional context for this specific posting.
The credit amount converted to the local currency of the company code for statutory reporting.
The debit amount converted to the local currency of the company code for statutory reporting.
The user ID or name of the person who last modified this journal entry document.
The date and time when this journal entry record was last modified in the system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
The percentage of completion used for revenue recognition entries under IFRS 15. Represents the stage of project completion at the time of this journal entry.
The user ID or name of the person who posted this journal entry to the general ledger, finalizing the transaction.
The date and time when this journal entry was posted to the general ledger. Represents the moment the entry became final and immutable.
The date on which the journal entry is posted to the general ledger. This is the principal business event timestamp for financial period assignment and reporting.
Two-digit code that controls the type of posting (debit or credit) and the account type (GL, customer, vendor, asset). Standard SAP posting keys include 40=GL Debit, 50=GL Credit.
The profit center to which revenues and costs are allocated for segment reporting and profitability analysis.
External reference number or identifier linking this journal entry to source documents such as invoices, purchase orders, or contracts.

The method used to calculate percentage of completion for revenue recognition. Cost-to-cost=based on costs incurred, Units-delivered=based on deliverables, Milestones=based on project milestones, Time-elapsed=based on time.
Flag indicating whether this journal entry is a reversal of a previous entry. True if this is a reversal document.
Code indicating the reason for reversal. 01=Error Correction, 02=Period-End Adjustment, 03=Accrual Reversal, 04=Reclassification, 05=Other.
The document number of the original journal entry that this entry reverses. Populated only when reversal_indicator is true.
The calculated tax amount for this journal entry line in the document currency.
The tax code applied to this journal entry line, determining the tax rate and tax account for automatic tax calculation.
The trading partner company code for intercompany transactions, used for consolidation and elimination entries.
The user ID or name of the person who created this journal entry document in the system.
Unique identifier for the journal entry line item. Primary key for this entity representing one leg of a double-entry accounting transaction.
Reference to the construction project to which this financial transaction is allocated. Links financial data to project master data for job costing and project P&L.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Journal entry lines need to be allocated to a cost center; replace the existing cost_center_code string with a proper foreign key.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: journal_entry_line has a cost_code STRING column but no FK to the cost_code master. Individual debit/credit line items in construction accounting are classified by cost code for granular job costing.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Journal entry line items reference GL accounts; FK provides authoritative account details.
Reference to the parent journal entry header that contains this line item. Links this line to the overall transaction batch and posting context.

Foreign key linking to workforce.timesheet_line. Business justification: Payroll journal line audit trail: individual journal entry lines for labor costs must be traceable to the specific timesheet line that originated them. This supports line-level payroll reconciliation
Reference to the vendor or subcontractor associated with this line item, if applicable. Used for accounts payable reconciliation and vendor spend analysis.

Foreign key linking to project.wbs_element. Business justification: Line-level WBS cost allocation: individual journal entry lines are coded to WBS elements for granular cost tracking. journal_entry_line.wbs_element_code is denormalized. This FK enables line-item WBS
The monetary amount of this journal entry line in the local reporting currency of the entity. Used for financial statement preparation and local statutory reporting.
The monetary amount of this journal entry line in the original transaction currency, before any currency conversion. Enables multi-currency accounting and foreign exchange analysis.
Additional reference field used for payment allocation, clearing, and reconciliation. Often contains check numbers, wire transfer references, or clearing document numbers.
The base amount before tax and adjustments, used for payment terms calculation and cash discount determination.
The business area or division to which this line item is allocated. Supports segment reporting and divisional P&L analysis.
The date on which this line item was cleared (matched with a payment or offsetting entry). Null if the item remains open.
The document number of the clearing transaction that closed this line item. Used for payment reconciliation and audit trail.
Indicates whether this line is a debit (D) or credit (C) entry in the double-entry accounting system.
The date on the source document (invoice, receipt, contract) that originated this journal entry line. May differ from posting date due to processing lag.
The payment due date for this line item, calculated based on payment terms and document date. Critical for cash flow management and aging reports.
The system timestamp when this journal entry line was created in the ERP system. Used for audit trail and transaction sequencing.
The foreign exchange rate applied to convert transaction currency to local currency. Critical for multi-currency consolidation and FX gain/loss calculation.
The fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year to which this journal entry line is assigned. Enables period-over-period financial analysis.
The fiscal year to which this journal entry line is assigned, based on the posting date and the organization's fiscal calendar.
The system timestamp when this journal entry line was last modified. Tracks changes for audit compliance and data lineage.
Free-text description or memo for this journal entry line, providing additional context for the transaction. Used for audit trail and user clarification.
Sequential line number within the journal entry, establishing the order of debit and credit legs within the transaction.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the local reporting currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
The payment terms code applicable to this line item, defining due dates and cash discount conditions. Used for cash flow forecasting and AP/AR aging.
The accounting date on which this journal entry line was posted to the general ledger. Determines the fiscal period for financial statement inclusion.
SAP-specific posting key that controls the account type (GL, vendor, customer) and debit/credit indicator. Used for system-level transaction control.
The profit center responsible for this line item. Enables profitability analysis and internal management reporting by business unit.
The source document number (invoice number, PO number, payment reference) that originated this journal entry line. Provides audit trail to source transaction.

The type of source document that originated this journal entry line. Classifies the transaction origin for audit and reconciliation purposes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: invoice|purchase_order|payment|receipt|adjustment|accrual|reversal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Flag indicating whether this line item is a reversal of a previous entry. Used for error correction tracking and audit compliance.
The document number of the original entry that this line item reverses. Provides audit trail for corrections and adjustments.
The tax amount associated with this line item in local currency. Supports tax reporting and reconciliation to tax authorities.
The tax code applied to this line item, determining the tax rate and tax account posting. Used for VAT, sales tax, and GST compliance reporting.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the original transaction currency.
Primary key for cost_center

Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: Required for Cost Center Allocation Report per client, enabling profitability analysis by linking each cost center to the client account it serves.

Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: cost_center has a company_code STRING column but no FK to the company_code master. In SAP controlling (CO), every cost center belongs to exactly one company code (legal entity). This is a fundamental

Identifier of the parent cost center in the organizational hierarchy. Enables roll-up reporting for regional, divisional, and corporate-level P&L (Profit and Loss) and EBITDA (Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization) analysis. Nullable for top-level cost centers.

The methodology used to allocate or distribute costs from this cost center to other cost objects (projects, WBS (Work Breakdown Structure) elements, or profit centers). Direct charge posts costs explicitly; activity-based uses cost drivers; headcount and square footage are common overhead allocation bases; revenue-based allocates proportionally to project revenue; none indicates no allocation (terminal cost center).

The approved annual or period budget allocated to this cost center. Used for budget vs. actual variance reporting, CPI (Cost Performance Index) calculation in EVM (Earned Value Management), and financial forecasting.

The time horizon for which the budget amount is defined. Annual budgets align with fiscal year planning; quarterly and monthly budgets support rolling forecasts; project lifecycle budgets span the duration of a construction project from NTP (Notice to Proceed) to handover.

The business segment or division to which this cost center is assigned (e.g., Infrastructure, Energy, Commercial, Residential). Used for segment reporting under IFRS 8 and internal divisional P&L (Profit and Loss) analysis.

Accounting classification of the cost center for financial treatment. Direct centers charge costs to specific projects or WBS (Work Breakdown Structure) elements; indirect centers allocate costs via overhead rates; overhead centers represent corporate G&A (General and Administrative); capitalized centers accumulate costs for asset creation; expensed centers flow directly to P&L (Profit and Loss).

The externally-known alphanumeric code identifying the cost center in financial systems and reports. Used in SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA (Cost Center Accounting) for posting and reporting. Typically follows organizational coding standards (e.g., project-based, regional, functional).

The SAP S/4HANA Controlling (CO) area to which this cost center belongs. Controlling areas represent organizational units for internal management accounting and can span multiple company codes. Used for cross-company cost center reporting and consolidation.

A user-defined grouping or classification for aggregating related cost centers in reports (e.g., all regional offices, all project cost centers for a specific client, all MEP (Mechanical Electrical and Plumbing) cost centers). Used for flexible reporting hierarchies beyond the standard organizational structure.

Current lifecycle status of the cost center. Active centers accept postings; inactive centers are temporarily disabled; planned centers are approved but not yet operational; closed centers are archived and no longer accept transactions; suspended centers are on hold pending review or restructuring.

Classification of the cost center by its primary business function. Project cost centers track job-specific costs; regional offices track geographic operations; corporate overhead captures non-project administrative costs; shared services represent centralized functions (IT, HR, Finance); support functions include PMO (Project Management Office), procurement, and QA/QC (Quality Assurance/Quality Control); operational centers track site-level or equipment-based activities.

The primary cost element group or cost category that this cost center typically incurs (e.g., labor, materials, equipment, subcontractor, overhead). Used for cost structure analysis and variance reporting by cost type.
The username or identifier of the system user who created this cost center record. Used for accountability, audit trails, and access control reviews.
The date and time when this cost center record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails, data lineage tracking, and compliance with financial record-keeping regulations.

The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which this cost center's budget and actuals are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Used for multi-currency consolidation and foreign exchange variance analysis.

Extended free-text description providing additional context about the cost center's purpose, scope, and responsibilities. Used for documentation, audit trails, and onboarding new finance team members.

The primary functional discipline or department this cost center supports. Used for cross-project functional cost analysis, overhead allocation, and shared-service chargeback models. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: engineering|procurement|construction|quality|safety|finance|hr|it|legal|admin — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

The depth of this cost center in the organizational hierarchy tree. Level 1 represents corporate or top-level centers; higher numbers represent nested project, site, or functional centers. Used for drill-down reporting and aggregation logic.

Indicates whether costs incurred in this cost center are billable to external clients or internal projects. True for project cost centers with reimbursable contracts; false for corporate overhead and non-billable support functions. Used for revenue recognition and progress billing logic.
The username or identifier of the system user who last modified this cost center record. Used for change accountability, audit trails, and data quality investigations.
The date and time when this cost center record was last updated. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and data quality monitoring.

Indicates whether this cost center is locked for new postings. True when the cost center is under audit, pending closure, or frozen for period-end reporting. Used to enforce financial controls and prevent unauthorized transactions.
The full descriptive name of the cost center, used for human-readable identification in financial reports, dashboards, and P&L (Profit and Loss) statements.

The standard overhead rate (as a percentage) applied to direct costs posted to this cost center. Used for indirect cost allocation, GMP (Guaranteed Maximum Price) contract pricing, and full absorption costing. Nullable for cost centers that do not apply overhead rates.

Three-letter code representing the geographic region or country where this cost center operates (e.g., USA, GBR, ARE). Used for regional P&L (Profit and Loss) reporting, tax jurisdiction mapping, and geographic segment analysis.

The physical site, office, or facility location associated with this cost center. Used for site-level cost tracking, field operations reporting, and HSE (Health Safety and Environment) cost allocation.
The date from which this cost center becomes effective and can accept financial postings. Used for time-dependent cost center master data and period-based reporting.
The date until which this cost center remains effective. Nullable for open-ended cost centers. Used to manage cost center lifecycle, project closeout, and organizational restructuring.
Primary key for cost_code
FK to finance.cost_center

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: cost_code has a gl_account_code STRING column but no FK to the gl_account master. In construction accounting, each cost code maps to a specific GL account for financial posting (e.g., CSI division 03

Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether cost postings to this code require additional approval workflow beyond standard posting authorization. Used for high-risk or high-value cost categories.
Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether this cost code can have budget allocated to it. Typically True for leaf-level codes and False for parent/rollup codes.

Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether costs posted to this code should be capitalized as assets on the balance sheet rather than expensed. Critical for Engineering Procurement and Construction (EPC) projects and asset-intensive developments.
The primary classification of the cost code into major expenditure categories. Used for high-level financial reporting and Profit and Loss (P&L) analysis.

The unique alphanumeric cost code identifier used across all project accounting systems. This is the externally-known business identifier used in SAP S/4HANA Project Systems and Viewpoint Vista for budget allocation and actual cost posting.

Current lifecycle status of the cost code. Active codes are available for budget allocation and cost posting. Inactive codes are retained for historical reporting but cannot be used for new transactions. Deprecated codes have been superseded by newer classifications.
Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether actual costs can be posted directly to this cost code. Typically True for detailed codes and False for summary/rollup codes.

Classification indicating whether the cost is directly attributable to a specific project deliverable (direct) or allocated across multiple activities (indirect/overhead). Critical for job costing and Earned Value Management (EVM) calculations.
The date and time when this cost code record was first created in the system. Part of the standard audit trail for master data management.
The two-digit CSI MasterFormat division number (00-49) that this cost code maps to. Provides standardized alignment with construction specifications and Bill of Quantities (BOQ) structures.
Detailed description of the cost code explaining the scope of work, materials, or services covered by this classification. Provides additional context beyond the name for proper cost allocation.

The engineering or construction discipline associated with this cost code (e.g., 'CIVIL', 'STRUCT', 'MECH', 'ELEC', 'INST' for Mechanical Electrical Plumbing (MEP) disciplines). Used for discipline-specific cost tracking and resource planning.
The date after which this cost code is no longer active for new transactions. Null for currently active codes. Used for cost code retirement and historical reporting.
The date from which this cost code becomes active and available for use in budget allocation and cost posting. Supports time-based cost code structures and phased project implementations.

Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether costs posted to this code are included in Earned Value Management (EVM) calculations including Cost Performance Index (CPI) and Schedule Performance Index (SPI) metrics.
The unique identifier for this cost code in the source system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA Cost Element number, Viewpoint Vista Cost Code ID). Used for cross-system reconciliation and integration.
The user ID or name of the person who last modified this cost code record. Used for change tracking and data governance.
The date and time when this cost code record was last modified. Part of the standard audit trail for master data management and change tracking.

The hierarchical level of this cost code within the Cost Breakdown Structure (CBS). Typically ranges from 1 (top-level category) to 4 (detailed line item). Level 1 might be 'Labor', Level 2 'Structural Labor', Level 3 'Steel Erection Labor'.

The full descriptive name of the cost code providing human-readable identification of the cost category (e.g., 'Structural Steel Erection', 'Concrete Formwork Labor', 'Electrical Conduit Installation').
Free-text field for additional notes, usage guidelines, or special instructions related to this cost code. Provides context for proper application and interpretation.
The cost code of the parent category in the hierarchical Cost Breakdown Structure (CBS). Null for top-level cost codes. Enables roll-up reporting and hierarchical budget analysis.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the standard rate (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP'). Required for multi-currency projects and international joint ventures.
The accounting method used for revenue recognition associated with this cost code. Percentage of completion is the most common method for construction projects under IFRS 15 and ASC 606.
Identifier of the operational system of record from which this cost code was originally sourced or is mastered. Supports multi-system integration and data lineage tracking.

The standard cost rate per unit of measure for this cost code. Used for budget estimation, variance analysis, and Earned Value Management (EVM) calculations. May represent labor rate per hour, material cost per unit, or equipment rate per day.
Code indicating the tax treatment for costs posted to this cost code (e.g., 'TAXABLE', 'EXEMPT', 'ZERO_RATED'). Used for Value Added Tax (VAT) and sales tax compliance in multi-jurisdiction projects.

The standard unit of measure for quantity tracking associated with this cost code (e.g., 'hours' for labor, 'cubic yards' for concrete, 'linear feet' for piping, 'each' for equipment). Used for productivity analysis and Material Take-Off (MTO) reconciliation.

Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether this cost code can be directly linked to Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) elements in Oracle Primavera P6 and SAP S/4HANA Project Systems for integrated schedule and cost management.
The user ID or name of the person who created this cost code record in the system. Used for audit trail and data governance.
Unique identifier for the job cost transaction record. Primary key for this entity.

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: job_cost_transaction has a gl_account_code STRING column but no FK to the gl_account master. Job cost transactions post to GL accounts for financial reporting (IFRS/GAAP compliance). Adding gl_account

Foreign key linking to project.phase. Business justification: Phase-level cost tracking: job cost transactions must be attributable to project phases for phase cost reporting and gate review financial analysis. Construction cost controllers require phase-level a
Reference to the original job cost transaction that this record reverses or corrects. Null for original transactions.

Foreign key linking to workforce.timesheet_line. Business justification: Labor job cost posting process: each posted timesheet line generates a job_cost_transaction. This FK enables labor cost audit traceability — auditors and project controllers must trace every job cost
Reference to the vendor or subcontractor who provided the goods or services for this transaction. Applicable for material, equipment rental, and subcontractor costs.

Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_invoice. Business justification: Construction job cost transactions are triggered by vendor invoice postings. A direct FK from job_cost_transaction to vendor_invoice enables cost-posting-to-source-invoice traceability for audit, thre
The approval workflow status of this cost transaction: pending (awaiting review), approved (validated and committed), or rejected (not accepted).
The name or identifier of the person who approved this cost transaction. Used for audit trail and accountability.
The date and time when this cost transaction was approved. Used for audit trail and workflow tracking.
The total cost converted to the project or corporate base currency for consolidated financial reporting and P&L analysis.
Indicates whether this cost is billable to the client (true) or non-billable internal cost (false). Used for progress billing and revenue recognition.
Indicates whether this cost has been included in a client invoice (true) or remains unbilled (false). Used for work-in-progress and revenue recognition tracking.
High-level classification of the cost transaction type: labor, material, equipment, subcontractor, overhead, or indirect costs.
The date and time when this cost transaction record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data governance.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency project accounting.
Free-text description of the cost transaction providing additional context about the work performed, materials used, or services rendered.
The exchange rate applied to convert the transaction amount to the project base currency. Null if transaction is in base currency.
The fiscal period (e.g., 2024-Q1, 2024-03) to which this transaction is assigned for financial reporting and period close.
The user or system that last modified this cost transaction record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
The date and time when this cost transaction record was last modified. Used for audit trail and data governance.
Additional free-text notes or comments about this cost transaction, including justifications, clarifications, or special handling instructions.
The date when the transaction was posted to the financial system. May differ from transaction_date due to approval or processing delays.
The quantity of the resource consumed or work performed (e.g., labor hours, material units, equipment hours). Used for unit cost analysis.
Indicates whether this transaction is a reversal or correction of a previously posted cost (true) or an original transaction (false).
The unique transaction identifier in the source system. Enables traceability back to the originating record for audit and reconciliation.
The total cost amount for this transaction (quantity × unit_cost). Represents the actual cost posted to the project for EVM ACWP and CPI calculations.
The business date when the cost was incurred or the work was performed. Used for period-based cost reporting and EVM actual cost (ACWP) calculations.
Business-facing unique transaction identifier or document number from the source system (e.g., timesheet number, invoice number, receipt number).
Current lifecycle status of the cost transaction: draft (pending approval), posted (committed to ledger), approved (validated), reversed (corrected), or cancelled.
The cost per unit of measure for this transaction. Used to calculate total cost and for rate variance analysis.
The unit in which the quantity is measured (e.g., hours, cubic meters, tons, each). Aligns with industry standard UOM codes.
The user or system that created this cost transaction record. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Unique identifier for the project budget record. Primary key for this entity.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Project budgets are associated with a cost center; replace the free‑text cost_center column with a foreign key.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Project budgets reference a cost code; replace the free‑text cost_code column with a foreign key to the cost_code master.
Reference to the prior version of this budget record if this is a revision. Enables historical tracking and variance analysis across budget versions.
Total actual costs incurred to date against this budget. Includes invoiced amounts and accrued costs. Used for EVM Actual Cost of Work Performed (ACWP).
Date when this budget record was formally approved by authorized stakeholders. Triggers budget activation and commitment authority.
Name or identifier of the person who approved this budget. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Cumulative sum of all approved change order adjustments to the budget. Includes client-approved scope changes and contract modifications.
Date when this budget was approved and baselined. Establishes the performance measurement baseline for EVM.
Unique business identifier for the budget record. Used for external reporting and cross-system reference.
Descriptive name of the budget line item or WBS budget element. Human-readable identifier for reporting.
Free-text notes and comments about this budget record. Captures assumptions, constraints, change justifications, and other contextual information.
Name or identifier of the project manager or cost manager responsible for managing this budget. Accountable for budget performance.
End date of the budget period this record applies to. Defines the time window for budget consumption and variance analysis.
Start date of the budget period this record applies to. Used for time-phased budget tracking and cash flow forecasting.
Current lifecycle status of the budget record. Approved budgets are baselined; active budgets are in use; frozen budgets are locked for change control.
Classification of the budget record. Contract budget represents client-approved amounts; internal budget represents target cost; contingency and management reserve represent risk buffers.
Unit of measure for quantity-based budgets (e.g., cubic meters for concrete, linear meters for piping, hours for labor). Used for unit rate calculations.
Difference between current approved budget and actual costs. Positive values indicate under-budget; negative values indicate over-budget.
Planned quantity of work or materials for this budget line. Used with unit rates to calculate total budget amounts.
Total value of purchase orders and subcontracts issued against this budget. Represents contractual obligations not yet invoiced.
Budget allocation for identified risks and known-unknowns. Managed by the project manager and released through change control process.
Reference to the specific contract line item or BOQ item this budget corresponds to. Links budget to contractual scope and payment terms.
Date and time when this budget record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this budget record. Supports multi-currency project accounting.
The current total approved budget including original budget plus all approved change orders. This is the active baseline for Earned Value Management (EVM) calculations.
Projected total cost at project completion based on current performance trends. Used for variance analysis and early warning of budget overruns.
Source of funding for this budget (e.g., client payment, internal investment, joint venture contribution, government grant). Used for cash flow and financial reporting.
Indicates whether this budget record is currently active and available for cost posting. Inactive budgets are closed or superseded.
Indicates whether this budget record represents the approved performance measurement baseline for EVM. True for baseline budgets used in BCWS calculations.
Date and time when this budget record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change for audit and synchronization purposes.
Budget allocation for unknown-unknowns and unidentified risks. Held at executive level and not part of the performance measurement baseline.
The initial approved budget amount at project baseline. Represents the starting contract value or internal target cost before any change orders.
Sequential version number for this budget record. Incremented with each approved change to maintain audit trail of budget evolution.
Cost per unit of measure for this budget item. Multiplied by budgeted quantity to derive total budget amount.
